concerns.text: FR-006 and SC-004 require Moving Block Bootstrap (MBB) with block length = 4 weeks. Task T034 implements MBB with block length = 1 quarter (3 months). This is a direct contradiction to the spec's success criteria, meaning the task cannot satisfy the requirement.

FR-012 and SC-006 require sensitivity analysis masking proportions to be defined (currently [deferred] in spec). Task T052 delegates the resolution of these '[deferred]' placeholders to a runtime task. The spec requires the pipeline to be fully automated and reproducible; deferring the definition of the masking range to runtime prevents the Success Criteria from being measurable or the pipeline from being deterministic without human intervention.

FR-005 requires NBER recession dates to be sourced from the 'NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee'. Task T036a fetches data from a generic landing page (https://www.nber.org/research/business-cycle-dating) rather than a specific, canonical CSV/JSON endpoint. This risks violating the Constitution's 'Data Hygiene' and 'Single Source of Truth' principles regarding checksummed, canonical data sources, as the landing page content is not a stable data artifact.

FR-014 requires validating sentiment drift against an external economic event timeline (concordance score). While T036 handles recession periods, there is no explicit task to compute the 'concordance score' (percentage of recessions where peak sentiment occurs ≤ 1 quarter before onset) as required by the spec's acceptance criteria for FR-014.

Task T029 creates a manual decision gate (write temp file -> read/decide -> fit) for the Johansen test logic. The spec (US-2) and Plan (Phase 1) require the system to automatically select the cointegration rank and model type (VAR vs VECM) based on trace/max-eigenvalue statistics. Fragmenting this into a manual decision step risks violating the Constitution's 'Reproducibility' principle which requires the pipeline to run end-to-end without manual intervention.
